consortiums.name: IPCEI-CIS / 8ra
consortiums.fullName: Important Project of Common European Interest — Cloud Infrastructure & Services
consortiums.role: Direct participant — FedEU.ai. Co-leads Workstream 3. Leads the AI Cluster.
consortiums.members: 19 direct participants: Deutsche Telekom, Telefónica España, Atos, Orange, SAP, Reply, TIM, Oktawave, Atende Industries, OpenNebula Systems, Arsys, Leaseweb, 4iG, E-Group, Tiscali, CloudFerro, Siemens, Fincantieri, Engineering Ingegneria Informatica. WS3 Budapest workshop attendees (hottest leads): CloudFerro, ENEA, Engineering, Infobip, Reply, Tiscali, TIM Group, Gdańsk University of Technology.
consortiums.notes: E-Group co-leads WS3 and leads the AI Cluster — a position of authority within the consortium. Most powerful warm network available.

consortiums.name: EU DOME
consortiums.fullName: Distributed Open Marketplace for Europe — Cloud & Edge Services
consortiums.role: Named consortium member — 38 organisations from 14 EU countries
consortiums.members: Coordinated by Engineering Ingegneria Informatica. Key co-members with E-Group: CloudFerro, Orange, IONOS, Outscale, Expert.AI, European Dynamics, Dawex, Tecnalia, Alastria, FIWARE Foundation, Ubiwhere, and 27 others. Full list confirmed from DOME launch documentation.
consortiums.notes: E-Group is a named DOME participant — confirmed in public consortium documentation. Engineering Ingegneria Informatica (DOME coordinator) is also E-Group's WS3 workshop attendee and IPCEI-CIS peer — triple connection.

consortiums.name: HeliX
consortiums.fullName: Health Ecosystem for Learning and Innovation Across Sovereign Data Spaces
consortiums.role: Spearhead / project leader — E-Group leads HeliX
consortiums.members: 14 partners from Portugal, Italy, Hungary and Poland. Confirmed: Health Cluster Portugal (coordinator), ULS de Coimbra, EIT Health InnoStars (WP5 lead), Prologica, Opvance. E-Group hosted 2nd General Assembly at Budapest HQ — March 10–11 2026.
consortiums.notes: E-Group spearheads HeliX. Budget: €9.15M. EIT Health InnoStars is gateway to 45+ EU health SMEs and EU funding streams. ULS de Coimbra is ideal for GraDPaLM medical AI pilot.

consortiums.name: eIDAS 2.0
consortiums.fullName: European Digital Identity Framework — MyD Wallet Implementation
consortiums.role: MyD Wallet implementation partner — Hungary's national EUDI Wallet builder
consortiums.members: The eIDAS 2.0 ecosystem spans 550+ organisations across 27 countries in four large-scale pilot consortia: EWC (EU Digital Identity Wallet Consortium — includes Visa Europe, Amadeus, Digidentity, Finnair, Yubico, Swedish Agency for Digital Government); POTENTIAL (140+ participants from 19 member states — includes Thales, major banks and government agencies); NOBID (Nordic-Baltic cross-border banking and identity pilot — includes financial institutions across Nordic countries); DC4EU (Digital Credentials for Europe — 80 organisations from 22 member states, includes Gataca, focuses on education credentials and social security). New pilots launched 2025: APTITUDE (travel and vehicle credentials) and WE BUILD (197 participants, business and payment use cases, includes Procivis). By December 2026 every EU member state must issue at least one EUDI Wallet — creating mandatory compliance demand across banking, healthcare, telecoms, transport and large online platforms.
consortiums.notes: E-Group built Hungary's MyD Wallet — making them one of only 27 national EUDI Wallet implementation teams in Europe. This is an extraordinary credential. Every bank, hospital, telecom, transport company and large online platform across the EU must accept the EUDI Wallet by 2027 — all of them are potential E-Group clients for implementation, integration and sovereign AI intelligence layered on top of identity infrastructure. Priority targets from the pilot ecosystems: Thales (in both POTENTIAL and NOBID), Visa Europe (EWC), Digidentity (EWC — digital identity specialist), Gataca (DC4EU — verifiable credentials), Nordic banks in NOBID consortium.

events.description: E-Group and 4iG both received IPCEI-CIS certification. Antal Kuthy and László Blénessy (4iG Deputy CEO) received together at Hungarian Ministry reception hosted by Secretary of State Gergely Fábián.

E-Group selected as Hungary representative for EU DOME federated cloud marketplace. Named alongside CloudFerro, Engineering Ingegneria Informatica, Orange, IONOS, Expert.AI, European Dynamics, Dawex, Tecnalia and 30+ others.

IPCEI-CIS kick-off meeting in Brussels. E-Group co-leads Workstream 3 and leads the AI Cluster. CloudFerro, ENEA, Engineering, Infobip, Reply, Tiscali, TIM Group, Gdańsk University attended E-Group's Budapest WS3 workshop.
CloudFerro received PE investment from Innova Capital. CEO Maciej Krzyżanowski stated ambition to become European champion of open source big data cloud computing.

eIDAS 2.0 entered into force (Regulation EU 2024/1183). Every EU member state must issue at least one EUDI Wallet by end of 2026. Banks, hospitals, telecoms and large online platforms must accept it by 2027. 550+ organisations across 27 countries already in pilot programmes.
events.source: Official Journal of the European Union, April 2024

events.description: HeliX project launched in Porto, Portugal. E-Group spearheads HeliX — Health Ecosystem for Learning and Innovation Across Sovereign Data Spaces. 14 partners from Portugal, Italy, Hungary and Poland. Budget €9.15M.
events.source: EIT Health InnoStars press release, November 2025

events.description: Infobip appointed Krešo Žmak as new Chief Innovation Officer with explicit mandate to accelerate AI adoption across their product portfolio.

HeliX second General Assembly hosted at E-Group Budapest HQ, 10-11 March 2026. All 14 partner organisations attended. 1:1 bilateral meetings plus full-day plenary. Strong personal relationships built across consortium.
Reply attended AWS Summit Paris 2026, Global Industrie 2026 and SiTL 2026 in one week, actively showcasing IPCEI-CIS project demos. Publicly promoting their IPCEI-CIS work at scale.

E-Group marketing control centre (Vigil) initiated. Network mapped: 22 companies, 14 key people, 4 consortiums. No external outreach has yet been made to any warm lead. László Blénessy (4iG) has not been contacted despite warm connection since December 2023.

Reply Group actively monetising IPCEI-CIS participation: converting demos to enterprise clients via AWS partnership, targeting European industrial IoT and manufacturing sectors. Actively showcasing at major EU events April 2026.

Engineering Ingegneria Informatica coordinating DOME consortium (value 1B+ EUR through 2026). Giovanni Frattini has decision-making authority across DOME technical, policy and commercial dimensions. Triple connection confirmed: DOME coordinator, IPCEI-CIS peer, Budapest WS3 attendee.

4iG holds significant Hungarian government AI infrastructure contracts and digital transformation projects 2024-2026. Laszlo Blenessy actively involved in government partnerships. EU funding component confirmed.

CloudFerro is active ESA Copernicus DIAS operator specialising in Earth observation cloud services and AI-enabled geospatial data processing. Direct synergy with E-Group AI capabilities for space data analytics.

Major EU banks and telecoms facing hard 2027 EUDI Wallet compliance deadline. Significant market opportunity for EUDI implementation partners. E-Group MyD Wallet experience positions them as one of very few qualified implementation experts in Europe.

Infobip launched AgentOS — an AI-native orchestration platform for autonomous customer communications. CINO Kreso Zmak stated AgentOS is the control layer where AI agents, data, channels and customer intent converge. Infobip is transforming from a CPaaS platform into an AI-first intelligent orchestration layer. They have integrated Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers enabling AI agents to interact with third-party systems autonomously.

Deutsche Telekom (T-Systems) demonstrated European Edge Continuum at MWC 2026 — first federated edge cloud connecting five major European operators (DT, Orange, Telefonica, TIM, Vodafone). T-Systems Chief Sovereign Officer Christine Knackfuss-Nikolic led the announcement. DT also submitted proposal for IPCEI-AI to extend EU AI sovereignty. T-Systems is actively building sovereign AI infrastructure and is a confirmed IPCEI-CIS active participant.
events.source: Deutsche Telekom press release, MWC Barcelona, March 2026

events.description: IPCEI-CIS Reference Architecture v1 officially published by OpenNebula Systems (chairing company). Co-authored with SAP, TIM, Deutsche Telekom and others. The architecture defines a federated cloud-edge ecosystem for AI, manufacturing, mobility and energy. Total investment: 1.2B EUR state aid plus 1.4B EUR private. Project runs to 2028. E-Group co-leads WS3 and the AI Cluster within this framework — a position of significant authority.
events.source: OpenNebula Systems / IPCEI-CIS official publication, January 2026

events.description: Fact8ra launched — first multi-provider AI-as-a-Service platform in Europe under IPCEI-CIS. Combines GPU compute from 8 EU member states (France, Germany, Italy, Latvia, Netherlands, Poland, Spain, Sweden). Supports private LLM deployment including Mistral and EuroLLM. This is the commercial output of IPCEI-CIS that E-Group's AI Cluster work feeds into directly.

Infobip appointed Kreso Zmak as first-ever Chief Innovation Officer (effective 1 Jan 2026) with explicit mandate to accelerate AI adoption across entire product portfolio and transform Infobip into an AI-first company. New CRO Ante Pamukovic also appointed to align revenue with AI strategy. Veselin Vukovic joins board as Chief Alliances Officer — key for partnership discussions.

competitors.name: Aleph Alpha
competitors.description: German sovereign AI company specialising in explainable AI for public sector and regulated industries. Direct competitor for GovTech and HealthTech AI contracts across EU.
competitors.differentiator: E-Group builds applications, not just models. Aleph Alpha provides the model layer but not the sovereign infrastructure, knowledge graph, or sector-specific applications E-Group delivers.
competitors.watchFor: Any German or CEE government contracts they win. Their PhariaAI platform is expanding into enterprise orchestration — direct overlap with E-Group's knowledge graph work.

competitors.name: Atos / Eviden
competitors.description: French IT giant positioning as EU trusted cloud provider under EUCS certification. One of the largest EU public sector IT suppliers. Competes on large EU project delivery.
competitors.differentiator: Atos is infrastructure and delivery. E-Group is AI intelligence and sovereign applications. They could be a partner as much as a competitor — E-Group's AI layer could run on Atos infrastructure.
competitors.watchFor: Any IPCEI-CIS adjacent contracts. Their Eviden AI brand is expanding — watch for them entering the knowledge graph space.

competitors.name: Scalefocus / Codeflow
competitors.description: Bulgarian IT company building Codeflow — a sovereign AI control plane combining Azure, StackIT and on-premise infrastructure. Expanding across DACH, Benelux, Nordics.
competitors.differentiator: Scalefocus is a Microsoft Azure integrator with a sovereignty wrapper. E-Group builds genuinely sovereign infrastructure from the ground up with no hyperscaler dependency.
competitors.watchFor: Their CEE expansion. If they move into Hungary or Poland they compete directly for the same enterprise clients.

competitors.name: Gataca
competitors.description: Spanish verifiable credentials and EUDI Wallet specialist. Participated in DC4EU pilot. CEO Irene Hernandez is publicly vocal on eIDAS implementation challenges.
competitors.differentiator: Gataca does identity only. E-Group built a full national EUDI Wallet implementation (MyD) and layers AI intelligence on top of identity infrastructure — a far more complete offer.
competitors.watchFor: Any move into CEE markets. They are technically strong and well-funded.

competitors.name: Evrotrust
competitors.description: Bulgarian electronic identity provider. Has prepared EUDI Wallet solutions but is blocked by lack of national legislation in Bulgaria. Positioned for CEE expansion.
competitors.differentiator: E-Group has a live national implementation (MyD Wallet, Hungary) — Evrotrust does not. E-Group is ahead by at least 18 months in production experience.
competitors.watchFor: If Bulgarian government unblocks legislation, Evrotrust becomes a regional competitor rapidly.

competitors.name: Engineering Ingegneria Informatica
competitors.description: DOME coordinator and IPCEI-CIS peer. ~12,000 staff. Major EU public sector IT supplier and consortium lead. Competes for the same large EU project contracts.
competitors.differentiator: Engineering is a delivery integrator. E-Group is an AI innovator with proprietary technology. The relationship should be partnership not competition — Engineering delivers, E-Group provides the AI layer.
competitors.watchFor: Any AI product they develop internally. Giovanni Frattini as CTO is technically ambitious — if Engineering builds their own knowledge graph capability, the partnership opportunity closes.

competitors.name: T-Systems
competitors.description: Deutsche Telekom subsidiary. Launched Open Telekom Cloud as a sovereign EU cloud alternative to US hyperscalers. Serves large EU enterprise with managed AI services.
competitors.differentiator: T-Systems is infrastructure at massive scale. E-Group is sovereign AI applications for specific sectors. Not direct competitors — potential channel partner for E-Group solutions on T-Systems infrastructure.
competitors.watchFor: Their IPCEI-CIS participation and any AI application layer they build on top of their cloud.

competitors.name: LightOn
competitors.description: French enterprise AI company specialising in private, on-premise LLM deployments for regulated industries. Strong in French public sector.
competitors.differentiator: LightOn provides the model. E-Group provides the full sovereign AI stack including knowledge graph, connectors, identity, and sector applications. Different layers of the same stack.
competitors.watchFor: Any CEE expansion or partnership with a CEE systems integrator.
